title: NOC 31209 – Other Professional Occupations in Health Diagnosing and Treating | GTR Immigration
description: Other Professional Occupations in Health Diagnosing and Treating under NOC 31209 are in strong demand across the healthcare sector in Canada. Post-pandemic healthcare expansion, an aging population, and increased emphasis on specialized therapeutic services have significantly strengthened employment prospects for internationally trained professionals in this category. With competitive salaries, structured licensing pathways, and multiple Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) opportunities, this occupation provides a viable and strategic route toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group31 – Professional occupations in healthSub-Major Group312 – Therapy and assessment professionalsMinor Group3120 – Therapy and assessment professionalsNOC Code31209

duties: This group performs some or all of the following duties:
Diagnose diseases, deformities, and injuries of the human foot
Treat patients using braces, casts, orthotic devices, physical therapy, and medications
Perform forefoot and subcutaneous soft-tissue surgery
Chiropodists and Diploma or First-Degree Trained Podiatrists
Provide orthotic treatments and physical therapy
Use natural therapies including acupuncture, spinal manipulation, hydrotherapy, herbal medicine, clinical nutrition, and homeopathy
Diagnose musculoskeletal, circulatory, and nervous system disorders
Provide manipulative therapy, prescribe medications, or perform surgery
employment_requirements: Four-year doctoral degree in podiatric medicine (typically U.S. or Quebec)
Medical residency required in Alberta and British Columbia
D.P.M. degree required in Quebec, Ontario, Alberta, and British Columbia
Three-year diploma in chiropody (Canada) or D.Pod.M. (UK)
License required in New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Alberta, and British Columbia
License required in Nova Scotia, Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Alberta, and British Columbia
Certification from Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Canada
salary_prospects: Annual salaries typically range from $27,500 to $107,800, depending on specialization, experience, and location.
job_outlook: The outlook remains stable to positive nationwide. Expansion demand, retirement replacement, and growth in integrative medicine contribute to sustained hiring.
Immigrants with recognized credentials and provincial licensing have a competitive advantage, particularly in metropolitan healthcare centres and underserved communities.
labour_market_demand: Healthcare professionals classified under NOC 31209 are employed in:
This balanced yet positive outlook indicates steady demand, especially in larger provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Quebec.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 31209?
Professionals in this occupation benefit from:
Direct eligibility through Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker – TEER 1)
High professional standards and regulatory structure
Long-term career growth and private practice opportunities
Canada’s healthcare system values internationally trained professionals, making this a strong immigration pathway for qualified applicants.
